description: Cost-optimization epic, candidate #2 (docs/prd/cost-optimization-candidates.md §2, owner-contributed). IDENTICAL to `production-baseline` in every other dimension -- same system prompt, same task prompt, same skill (skill_01H2qu83NwnJ5zqcbrqsCcJ6 @ 1783340601977967), same tools, same (empty) parameters -- with EXACTLY ONE change: the backing model is Claude Haiku 4.5 (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) instead of Sonnet 5. This is the simplest possible cost lever (swap the model, change nothing else) and the highest quality risk, since the tasks this pipeline leans on hardest -- research source selection, cross-outlet dedup judgment, and hallucination avoidance -- are exactly where a materially less capable model is most likely to regress. Content-generation only: like every candidate on this topology it holds NO AWS/delivery role (FR-1), is a SEPARATE newly-created agent resource (its own agent_id, written once at first sync, NOT the baseline's), and can never fan out to subscribers. Its only delivery-side contact is the read-only GET /recent-briefs route (recent priors), never POST /deliver.
schedule_intent: none -- cost-optimization eval candidate only; never scheduled and never promoted to production without a separate, explicitly-approved cut-over decision
